reviews.review_msg: Seems to be a nice lady generally, however, when it comes to marking assignments or discussion posts, she is tough. Professor Arab does not give any relevant feedback at all and took forever to get our marks back. If you have no reason to take this course, simply don't. I don't recommend her if there are other available profs.
Probably the worst professor at SFU. Do not take this class.

Dr. Arab is great! She chose really interesting plays for us to study and every lecture approached the key themes from cool perspectives. Grading is done by your TA so it depends (my TA, Felix, was awesome! Assignments aren't awful, since the prompts are stuff that we focus on in class. Overall, great course!

Dr. Arab is underrated on here. She's awesome! Really interesting way of approaching the texts and analyzes them in a way that engages you the whole lecture! She chose great plays for us to study and lectures are really thought provoking. Assignments are what you'd expect from an english class, few papers but interesting topics! Love her!

Ronda made Shakespeare enjoyable. She picked a great selection of plays and focused on the texts themselves, rather than critical theory. This lecture-style made the task of talking about Shakespeare less daunting. Her papers were typical of the third year level. She gives you a choice on what to write on. Not a difficult marker, just picky.

This was one of my favourite classes I ever took. Ronda was such a good lecturer and fantastic at creating student discussion. She came well prepared with engaging lecture content and was always very clear in her expectations. She answers emails fast and encouraged people to come to office hours, where she gave very good feedback on papers.

I like her teaching method, she shows you movie clips during lecture that relate to the books. She tells you exactly what vocab and quotes to study, so it was easy to prepare for her final exam. My TA was great too! Great choice of books, good TAs, and  a prof who clearly outlines what's going to be on the exam! I recommend taking her class!

I thought she was a great prof! I hardly attended lectures and yet still did well on essays. The exam was straight forward, one of the easiest exams I've ever written. Reading material was simple too, and she clarified alot of info. Overall, I would recommend!

Clearly interested in what she teaches, but you may find it dull and dry if you're not interested in English literature. Otherwise, her lectures are very clear and I have learned a lot from her class. Few complaints here. Only one would be her slight lack of organization, but it isn't a significant issue.
I really enjoyed her ENG 103 lecture and tutorial. My writing capabilities improved vastly thanks to her encouragement and feedback.

Good prof, she tells you what kind of questions are going to be on the final (quotes from lectures, certain stage diagrams, etc) and is clear and straightforward. Involves students in discussions every class. Tutorial attendance counts for 15%, which really helped!
Promoted discussion in lecture and brought up/clarified points made by students. Very helpful, a little dry. Overall a good professor!
Lectures are all over the place. She's very hard to understand and I found myself completely zoning out the entire lecture most of the time. Avoid her if you can.
Avoid her if you can. Terrible professor.
She's really nice and helpfull one-on-one, but the lectures are painfully disorganized and hard to follow.
